h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The exchange proceeds linearly with the learner repeating and confirming the tutor's instructions for placing the time signature.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner only accepts, repeats, and follows instructions without performing any new device or criterion.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no pressure for reversal, and the tutor simply continues the established step-by-step confirmation route.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, and the tutor merely continues the prior lesson plan to close the scene.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner only accepts, repeats, and follows instructions without performing any new device or criterion.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, and the tutor merely continues the prior lesson plan to close the scene.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: There is no pressure for reversal, and the tutor simply continues the established step-by-step confirmation route.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: There is no pressure for reversal, and the tutor simply continues the established step-by-step confirmation route.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The exchange proceeds linearly as the learner correctly follows the tutor's step-by-step prompts.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Normal goes here too, at the contact. Up on the cart, perpendicular to the table.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully applies the newly established contact-based representation and direction criterion to a new force without needing step-by-step guidance.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no learner resistance, breakdown, or mismatch to trigger a tutor strategy reversal.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn to which the tutor could contingently adapt.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Normal goes here too, at the contact. Up on the cart, perpendicular to the table.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully applies the newly established contact-based representation and direction criterion to a new force without needing step-by-step guidance.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn to which the tutor could contingently adapt.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: There is no learner resistance, breakdown, or mismatch to trigger a tutor strategy reversal.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: There is no learner resistance, breakdown, or mismatch to trigger a tutor strategy reversal.
